LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, LP AND TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT MANAGEMENT, LLC'S PETITION TO VACATE OR CORRECT ARBITRATION AWARD. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THE AWARD WAS PROCURED BY "FRAUD OR UNDUE MEANS" OR THAT THE "ARBITRATOR EXCEEDED HIS POWERS..." AS SET FORTH IN CCP SECTION 1286.2. NONE OF THE ALLEGED ERRORS ARE EVIDENT ON THE FACE OF THE AWARD THAT WOULD AFFORD RELIEF UNDER CCP SECTION 1286.6. THE AWARD IS CONFIRMED. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED IT DID CONSIDER THE SEVERTSON CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, LP, AND TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT MANAGEMENT, LLC'S NOTICE OF: 1) PETITIONER'S INTENTION TO MOVE FOR NEW TRIAL AND 2) HEARING ON PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR A NEW TRIAL AND MOTION TO AMEND ORDER CONFIRMING ARBITRATION AWARD OR, ALTERNATIVELY, TO VACATE ORDER AND ENTER DIFFERENT ORDER. MOTION DENIED. REPLY IS STRICKEN AS VIOLATIVE OF MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL PROCEDURES. (CRC 3.1600, CCP SECTION 661). THE ARBITRATOR'S AWARD DOES NOT CONSTITUTE DAMAGES OR A MONEY JUDGMENT, BUT RATHER THE CREATION OF AN EXECUTORY CONTRACT. FURTHERMORE, THE AMOUNT WAS NOT VESTED AS OF THE TIME OF THE ARBITRATION AWARD. THE REQUEST TO SPECIFY THE AMOUNT TO BE AWARDED TO EACH OF THE PETITIONERS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of: 1) Petitioner'S Intention To Move For New Trial And 2) Hearing On Petitioner'S Mtn For A New Trial And Mtn To Amend Order Confirming Arbitration Award Or, Alt., To Vacate Order And Enter Different Order
Off Calendar Sep-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONERS TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, LP, AND TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT MANAGEMENT, LLC'S NOTICE OF: 1) PETITIONER'S INTENTION TO MOVE FOR NEW TRIAL AND 2) HEARING ON PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR A NEW TRIAL AND MOTION TO AMEND ORDER CONFIRMING ARBITRATION AWARD OR, ALTERNATIVELY, TO VACATE ORDER AND ENTER DIFFERENT ORDER. Appearances: James L. Goldman of Pircher Nichols & Meeks (310.201.8958) for petitioner and moving party. No other appearances. Counsel did not comply with SF Local Rule 8.3(D), and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Reply is stricken as violative of motion for new trial procedures. (CRC 3.1600, CCP section 661). The arbitrator's award does not constitute damages or a money judgment, but rather the creation of an executory contract. Furthermore, the amount was not vested as of the time of the arbitration award. The request to specify the amount to be awarded to each of the petitioners is denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

[Redacted] Version Of Notice Of Hearing On Pettiion To Vacate Or Correect Arbitration Award;

PETITIONER TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, L.P'S [Redacted] Version Of Pettiion To Vacate Or Correct Arbitration Award IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 3 0 2 PER JUDGE BUSCH'S RECUSAL. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Mtn To File Under Seal
Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
RESPONDENTS AND PETITIONERS JOINT, UNOPPOSED MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL.

APPEARANCES: James L. Goldman, Esq. of Pircher, Nichols & Meeks 310/201-8958 for petitioners; Martin S. Schenker, Esq. 693-2154 & Benjamin H. Kleine, Esq. 693-2022 of Cooley LLP for respondents.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Order signed in open court. In addition, the Court grants respondents & petitioners supplemental joint unopposed motion to file under seal, order signed. (The Court also signs an order adopting tentative ruling of May 25, 2010 granting motion to have records filed by petitioner in support of petition to vacate or, alternatively, to correct arbitration award placed under seal.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PETITIONERS TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, LP and TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT MANAGEMENT, LLC'S PETITION TO VACATE OR CORRECT ARBITRATION AWARD.

APPEARANCES: James L. Goldman, Esq. of Pircher, Nichols & Meeks 310/201-8958 for petitioners & moving parties; Martin S. Schenker, Esq. 693-2154 & Benjamin H. Kleine, Esq. of Cooley LLP for respondents & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: denied. Insufficient showing that the award was procured by "fraud or undue means" or that the "arbitrator exceeded his powers..." as set forth in CCP section 1286.2. None of the alleged errors are evident on the face of the award that would afford relief under CCP section 1286.6. The award is confirmed. In addition, the Court indicated it did consider the Severtson case. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
